fundamentals.name: MongoDB, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: MongoDB, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, provides general purpose database platform worldwide. The company offers MongoDB Atlas, a hosted multi-cloud database-as-a-service solution; MongoDB Enterprise Advanced, a commercial database server for enterprise customers to run in the cloud, on-premises, or in a hybrid environment; and Community Server, a free-to-download version of its database, which includes the functionality that developers need to get started with MongoDB. It offers professional services comprising consulting and training. The company was formerly known as 10gen, Inc. and changed its name to MongoDB, Inc. in August 2013. MongoDB, Inc. was incorporated in 2007 and is headquartered in New York, New York.

judgment.summary: MongoDB shows strong revenue growth and free cash flow but is unprofitable on a net basis and carries significant valuation multiples.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 12.1%; next earnings date is 2026-05-28.
positives: Revenue growth 26.7% YoY indicates strong market adoption.
Free cash flow of $463.32M TTM demonstrates operational efficiency.
Analyst consensus is a buy with a mean target suggesting upside potential.
negatives: Profit margin is negative at -2.9%, indicating net losses.
Operating margin is 0.0%, showing no operating profit.
Forward P/E of 43.40 and PEG ratio of 1.67 suggest a high valuation.
Debt/Equity ratio of 2.13 is relatively high.

news.paragraph: MongoDB is set to report its Q1 earnings on May 28th, with recent analyst optimism and AI momentum cited as potential drivers for its share price, which has rallied 75% over the past year. The company's Instruqt platform was also highlighted for its use in training AI practitioners at Google Cloud. While valuation appears high with a forward P/E of 43.40, analysts maintain a consensus buy rating with a mean target of $358.82.
